case_id: adult males aged 20 and over, all race and Hispanic-origin groups

pi_items.source: CDC NHANES 2015-2018 Table 11: Height in centimeters for adult males aged 20 and over. Official data reports mean = 175.3 cm (50th percentile/median = 175.4 cm)

Derived from CDC NHANES 2015-2018 Table 11 percentiles: 25th=170.1, 75th=180.2, 5th=162.8, 95th=187.4. IQR method: (180.2-170.1)/1.35=7.48 cm. 75th percentile method: (180.2-175.3)/0.674=7.26 cm. Task value 7.2 is consistent with 75th percentile derivation.
Mathematical property of normal distribution: P(μ-σ ≤ X ≤ μ+σ) = 68.27% ≈ 68%
Mathematical property of normal distribution: P(μ-2σ ≤ X ≤ μ+2σ) = 95.45% ≈ 95%
Mathematical property of normal distribution: P(μ-3σ ≤ X ≤ μ+3σ) = 99.73% ≈ 99.7%
Arbitrary verifier tolerance setting; not a data claim

privileged_info_summary: The task data is sourced from real CDC NHANES 2015-2018 survey data for US adult males aged 20 and over. The actual mean height is 175.3 cm with standard deviation approximately 7.3-7.5 cm derived from percentile data. The task uses mean=175.4 cm (which matches the median/50th percentile of the dataset) and SD=7.2 cm (which appears derived from the 75th percentile calculation, yielding 7.26 cm rounded to 7.2). All sigma bounds are mathematically correct given the stated parameters. The Empirical Rule percentages (68%, 95%, 99.7%) are statistically accurate for a normal distribution.
